caption: Basic Metadata
context: 1) Users should be able to create new documents.

caption: Deficient Solution
context: Let's pretend on each edit the users send their entire document over to a Document Service. The Document Service will then store the document in a blob storage system like S3.

caption: Operation Transforms
context: OT is low memory and fast, but comes with a big tradeoff.

caption: Document Service with Operational Transformation
context: Good Solution: Conflict-free Replicated Data Types (CRDTs)

caption: Sending Updates to Clients
context: Regardless of the ordering, by applying OT to the operations we can guarantee that each user sees the same document!

caption: Websocket Connections
context:
Pattern: Real-time Updates
This pattern of needing push updates to clients across a scaled backend service is a quintessential example of the real-time updates pattern.
Learn This Pattern

caption: Architecture with Consistent Hash
context: Since all of the users of a document are connecting to the same server, when updates happen we can simply pass them to all connected clients.

caption: Compaction Service
context: If the Document Service has a loaded document, it will discard the flip command to defer compaction which might corrupt existing operations.

caption: Online Snapshot/Compact Operations
